data.content: Now let me read all the source files. I'll call all the view commands in parallel:

Perfect! I have successfully read all source files in the ContosoUniversity project. Here's a complete summary of all the files:

## **CONTROLLERS**

### BaseController.cs
Abstract base controller providing shared functionality for database context and notification service across all controllers.

### CoursesController.cs
Manages course CRUD operations with support for teaching material image uploads (5MB max, image formats only). Includes file upload/deletion logic and entity notifications.

### DepartmentsController.cs
Handles department management with concurrency control using row versions. Supports department creation, editing, and deletion with notifications.

### HomeController.cs
Provides home page, about page (showing enrollment statistics), contact page, and error handling views.

### InstructorsController.cs
Manages instructor information including hire dates, office assignments, and course assignments. Supports complex course assignment operations via checkboxes.

### NotificationsController.cs
API controller for retrieving pending notifications from an in-memory queue and marking them as read.

### StudentsController.cs
Handles student CRUD operations with pagination (10 per page), search, and sorting by name/enrollment date. Includes validation for enrollment dates.

## **MODELS**

### Course.cs
Entity representing a course with CourseID (non-generated), Title, Credits (0-5), Department reference, and optional teaching material image path.

### CourseAssignment.cs
Junction table connecting instructors to courses (many-to-many relationship).

### Department.cs
Department entity with name, budget (money type), start date, instructor administrator assignment, and row version for concurrency control.

### Enrollment.cs
Enrollment entity linking students to courses with optional letter grades (A-F).

### ErrorViewModel.cs
Simple view model for error pages with RequestId property.

### Instructor.cs
Inherits from Person, adds hire date, office assignment, and course assignments.

### Notification.cs
Entity for storing entity change notifications (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE) with timestamps and read status.

### OfficeAssignment.cs
One-to-one relationship with Instructor, stores office location.

### Person.cs
Abstract base class for Instructor and Student with ID, LastName, FirstMidName, and computed FullName property.

### Student.cs
Inherits from Person, adds enrollment date and enrollments collection.

### SchoolViewModels/AssignedCourseData.cs
ViewModel for displaying course assignment UI with selected/unselected state.

### SchoolViewModels/EnrollmentDateGroup.cs
ViewModel for grouping students by enrollment date in statistics.

### SchoolViewModels/InstructorIndexData.cs
ViewModel aggregating instructors, courses, and enrollments for instructor index view.

## **DATA**

### DbInitializer.cs
Static initializer seeding database with 8 students, 5 instructors, 4 departments, 7 courses, office assignments, and course-instructor assignments with sample enrollments.

### SchoolContext.cs
DbContext configuring all entities, Table-per-Hierarchy (TPH) inheritance for Person, DateTime to datetime2 conversion, and relationship mappings.

### SchoolContextFactory.cs
Factory for creating SchoolContext with connection string and retry policies.

## **SERVICES**

### NotificationService.cs
In-memory notification queue service using ConcurrentQueue with max size of 500 notifications. Generates human-readable messages for entity operations.

## **OTHER**

### PaginatedList.cs
Generic pagination helper extending List<T> with PageIndex, TotalPages, and HasPreviousPage/HasNextPage properties.

### Program.cs
Application startup configuration setting up Entity Framework, MVC, static file serving for wwwroot, Content, and Scripts folders, database initialization, and default routing.

result.content:
Perfect! I have successfully read all source files in the ContosoUniversity project. Here's a complete summary of all the files:

## **CONTROLLERS**

### BaseController.cs
Abstract base controller providing shared functionality for database context and notification service across all controllers.

### CoursesController.cs
Manages course CRUD operations with support for teaching material image uploads (5MB max, image formats only). Includes file upload/deletion logic and entity notifications.

### DepartmentsController.cs
Handles department management with concurrency control using row versions. Supports department creation, editing, and deletion with notifications.

### HomeController.cs
Provides home page, about page (showing enrollment statistics), contact page, and error handling views.

### InstructorsController.cs
Manages instructor information including hire dates, office assignments, and course assignments. Supports complex course assignment operations via checkboxes.

### NotificationsController.cs
API controller for retrieving pending notifications from an in-memory queue and marking them as read.

### StudentsController.cs
Handles student CRUD operations with pagination (10 per page), search, and sorting by name/enrollment date. Includes validation for enrollment dates.

## **MODELS**

### Course.cs
Entity representing a course with CourseID (non-generated), Title, Credits (0-5), Department reference, and optional teaching material image path.

### CourseAssignment.cs
Junction table connecting instructors to courses (many-to-many relationship).

### Department.cs
Department entity with name, budget (money type), start date, instructor administrator assignment, and row version for concurrency control.

### Enrollment.cs
Enrollment entity linking students to courses with optional letter grades (A-F).

### ErrorViewModel.cs
Simple view model for error pages with RequestId property.

### Instructor.cs
Inherits from Person, adds hire date, office assignment, and course assignments.

### Notification.cs
Entity for storing entity change notifications (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE) with timestamps and read status.

### OfficeAssignment.cs
One-to-one relationship with Instructor, stores office location.

### Person.cs
Abstract base class for Instructor and Student with ID, LastName, FirstMidName, and computed FullName property.

### Student.cs
Inherits from Person, adds enrollment date and enrollments collection.

### SchoolViewModels/AssignedCourseData.cs
ViewModel for displaying course assignment UI with selected/unselected state.

### SchoolViewModels/EnrollmentDateGroup.cs
ViewModel for grouping students by enrollment date in statistics.

### SchoolViewModels/InstructorIndexData.cs
ViewModel aggregating instructors, courses, and enrollments for instructor index view.

## **DATA**

### DbInitializer.cs
Static initializer seeding database with 8 students, 5 instructors, 4 departments, 7 courses, office assignments, and course-instructor assignments with sample enrollments.

### SchoolContext.cs
DbContext configuring all entities, Table-per-Hierarchy (TPH) inheritance for Person, DateTime to datetime2 conversion, and relationship mappings.

### SchoolContextFactory.cs
Factory for creating SchoolContext with connection string and retry policies.

## **SERVICES**

### NotificationService.cs
In-memory notification queue service using ConcurrentQueue with max size of 500 notifications. Generates human-readable messages for entity operations.

## **OTHER**

### PaginatedList.cs
Generic pagination helper extending List<T> with PageIndex, TotalPages, and HasPreviousPage/HasNextPage properties.

### Program.cs
Application startup configuration setting up Entity Framework, MVC, static file serving for wwwroot, Content, and Scripts folders, database initialization, and default routing.

- **HomeController**: Index, About, Contact, Error, and Unauthorized actions
- **InstructorsController**: Index, Details, and Create operations
- **NotificationsController**: GetNotifications, MarkAsRead, and Index

For testing the controllers with EF Core, I need to account for some implementation details: BaseController instantiates NotificationService directly rather than injecting it, the static queue in NotificationService could cause test interference, and ViewBag usage requires proper ControllerContext setup. I'll use xUnit with Moq for mocking dependencies like IWebHostEnvironment and ILogger, plus the InMemory EF Core provider for the database.

Setting up the test project structure...

Now I'm planning the test file organization with helper utilities for context creation, individual test classes for each controller and service, and tests for the PaginatedList and model classes. For the SchoolContext setup, I can pass InMemory DbContextOptions directly to the constructor, and I'll need to mock the dependencies that CoursesController requires.

Since the project targets .NET 10 RC1, I need to use matching prerelease packages for EF Core and the InMemory provider. I'll create the test project using `dotnet new xunit` and add the necessary packages, then start generating the test files.

Now I'm mapping out the test coverage I need: PaginatedList tests for pagination logic across different page scenarios, NotificationService tests for message queuing and notification handling, model tests for computed properties and enums, and controller tests starting with the Index action.

For StudentsController, I need to cover sorting variations (by name ascending and descending, by date in both directions), search filtering, pagination, and then the full CRUD operations with edge cases like null IDs and invalid model states. Similar coverage for CoursesController with its Index, Details, and Create endpoints.

Now I'm mapping out test cases for CoursesController that include image validation—checking for invalid file extensions and size limits—plus the standard CRUD operations. Then moving through DepartmentsController with its complete set of Index, Details, Create, Edit, and Delete scenarios including not found cases. HomeController has basic action tests, and I'm starting to outline InstructorsController tests.

For NotificationsController, I need to test the GetNotifications method with both empty and populated queues, plus MarkAsRead and Index. The tricky part is that NotificationService uses a static queue, so tests will interfere with each other unless I drain it between test runs. I'll handle that in the test setup before writing the actual test code.

I also realized that CoursesController and StudentsController use `.Single()` which throws an exception if the record isn't found, so the null checks in those methods are unreachable. I'll focus my tests on the happy paths and null ID cases rather than testing for Single() exceptions, since that's an implementation detail.
